sentence_reviews.text: Tokenization and serialization convert language, code, images, audio, actions, or records into packed sequences that model runtimes can consume.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Reviewed as synthesis across cited source pointers and, where applicable, the canonical atlas graph context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Former ambiguity resolved by narrowing the audit interpretation to synthesis support rather than requiring exact-source wording.

sentence_reviews.text: combined with Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML preprocessing discrete/text data and word embeddings; AIMA lexicon/grammar, word representations, and language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Tokenization directly concerns tokens and vocabularies; wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units, Information theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Tokenization, serialization & packing is modeled as depending on Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units, Information theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Large language models.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
